long_description: The main generator fails in flight, forcing transfer to battery or emergency buses and reducing available electrical loads.
pilot_actions: Confirm power source transfer, shed nonessential avionics, minimize sensor and mission loads, and land before backup power margins are exhausted.

long_description: An in-flight main electrical bus fault causes intermittent or sustained loss of power to multiple downstream avionics or aircraft systems.
pilot_actions: Reset affected bus only if checklist permits, prioritize essential flight instruments, disable nonessential equipment, and terminate the mission.

long_description: Emergency or battery-backed bus voltage decays in flight, threatening essential standby instruments, restart capability, or backup flight-system availability.
pilot_actions: Shed all nonessential loads, prioritize recovery and landing, avoid unnecessary system cycling, and assume reduced backup endurance.

long_description: A converter-rectifier unit fails in flight, reducing DC power quality and causing resets or dropouts in dependent avionics.
pilot_actions: Shed affected mission equipment, monitor for recurring avionics resets, and recover before essential displays or controls become unstable.

long_description: A wiring short or intermittent harness fault causes localized electrical dropouts, nuisance cautions, or transient system behavior in flight.
pilot_actions: Avoid unnecessary switching, isolate nonessential loads if checklist permits, and treat repeated transients as a cue to terminate the mission.

long_description: An AC distribution bus fault occurs in flight, removing power from multiple aircraft functions and forcing reconfiguration to remaining sources.
pilot_actions: Prioritize essential systems, avoid unnecessary resets, and recover before the remaining power architecture is overloaded.

long_description: An inverter fails in flight, degrading AC supply quality to equipment that depends on converted power from the DC system.
pilot_actions: Monitor instrument and avionics stability, shed affected nonessential loads, and land before degraded converted power affects essentials.

long_description: Electrical bus-tie control fails in flight, preventing normal load sharing or reconfiguration after another power-system fault.
pilot_actions: Avoid provoking additional electrical transients, keep loads conservative, and terminate the sortie with the simplified power configuration.

long_description: The generator control unit behaves incorrectly in flight, causing unstable voltage regulation, nuisance trips, or repeated generator disconnects.
pilot_actions: Minimize electrical load changes, avoid cycling the generator repeatedly, and recover if bus stability cannot be maintained.

long_description: The essential electrical bus is compromised in flight, threatening core flight instruments, control support, or critical avionics availability.
pilot_actions: Shed everything nonessential immediately, focus on basic aircraft recovery, and land without delay before essential power is lost.
